response.justification: The candidate is a versatile, hands-on senior engineer with ~15 years of broad experience across web, mobile, and embedded systems, plus genuine high-throughput systems work at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic. However, the role is a Principal IC6 focused on experimentation and growth platforms, and the resume shows no experimentation, A/B testing, feature-flagging, telemetry, or metrics-platform experience, nor evidence of principal-level scope such as leading multi-team, multi-workstream architectural initiatives. Most recent roles are senior/staff IC positions at small companies with product-focused work, which falls well short of the required demonstrated leadership in platform systems used across many teams.
strengths: Broad full-stack experience across many languages and stacks (TypeScript/Node, D, C/C++, Java)
Real high-scale, low-latency systems experience handling millions of HTTP requests per second
Strong engineering craft: TDD, CI/CD, open-source authorship, teaching and community workshops
concerns: No experimentation, feature-flagging, telemetry, or growth-measurement platform experience
No demonstrated principal-level scope leading multi-team architecture or cross-org initiatives
Limited evidence of statistical/data-analysis depth required for experimentation quality and bias detection

key_factors.factor: Seniority gap vs Principal IC6 scope
